question_relevance_notes.linguistic: Judge correctly identified that linguistic evidence was missing and asked the linguistic agent to search for text.
question_relevance_notes.regulatory: Judge correctly targeted the regulatory agent to look for finer details to distinguish between right-hand traffic countries.
question_relevance_notes.meta: Judge correctly targeted the meta agent to verify infrastructure details (utility poles) which are often region-specific.

response_update_notes.linguistic: Agent correctly reported no text found, which is a definitive negative result.
response_update_notes.regulatory: Agent provided specific details about edge line width and lack of reflectors, which are new observations.
response_update_notes.meta: Agent provided specific details about the pole shape (tapered) and cross-arm type, which are new observations.

verdict.judge_question_strategy_notes: The judge correctly identified that the initial evidence (double yellow lines, right-hand traffic) was shared by multiple countries (Brazil, Mexico, Colombia, etc.). The strategy to ask the regulatory agent for finer details (reflectors, line width) and the meta agent for infrastructure specifics (pole design) was highly effective and targeted the right domains.
verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge effectively synthesized the high-confidence assessments from landscape, regulatory, and meta agents to confirm Brazil. The reasoning was sound and directly supported by the agent responses.
verdict.discussion_convergence_notes: The discussion successfully narrowed down the candidates. The initial broad set of candidates was reduced to a high-confidence consensus on Brazil through targeted follow-up questions.
